behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing; missing fields return one clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is validator-to-orchestrator handoff: the system must both aggregate missing fields and block downstream modules. Unit could miss accidental downstream invocation. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is local validation and gating only, with no real OS/network/subprocess or model dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestration with payloads missing multiple required fields; assert returned error lists every missing key and assert insurance/triage/scheduling/confirmation modules are not invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, >150-year-old dates, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Single deterministic validation rule set on one input field; no module boundary or I/O required.
behaviors.test_description: Call DOB validator directly with representative valid/invalid cases (future, too old, malformed string) and assert exact validation outcomes.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code to payer API and interprets eligible/not eligible/pending.
behaviors.justification: Needs module wiring between eligibility service and payer client to verify request construction and response mapping. Rejected System: behavior is contract/mapping and can be caught with a controlled stub endpoint/client; no requirement for real payer infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub payer endpoint/client capturing outbound payload; assert patient ID and plan code are correct; return each status variant and verify internal eligibility mapping.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is set to pending and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Failure is cross-module error propagation and fallback policy (payer client error -> intake state). Can be reproduced with deterministic transport error injection. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network conditions, only unreachable handling semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Inject timeout/connection failure from payer client during intake; assert eligibility becomes pending and scheduling/confirmation path still runs.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: This is LLM-call orchestration/payload correctness, not model judgment quality. AI-CALL default is disqualified by behavior text focusing exclusively on model input payload fields.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage service with stub model client and known config; inspect sent payload to confirm symptoms and emergency rubric are both present and correctly serialized.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage marks emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath) as high urgency.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model medical-risk judgment; deterministic stubs would hide the exact failure class.
behaviors.test_description: Execute triage against live model on curated emergency symptom cases; assert urgency is high and track pass rate across repeated runs to account for non-determinism.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field, not only a numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Requires parser-to-persistence/schema handoff verification. Unit parser test alone can miss dropped field during storage mapping. Rejected Agentic: behavior is about storing returned field presence, not evaluating model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response containing score + explanation through parser/storage path; assert persisted record contains non-empty explanation column/field alongside score.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation is case-specific and includes symptom-based rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This tests output quality/grounding of real model explanations. Stubs cannot reveal generic-but-plausible live model failure.
behaviors.test_description: Send multiple distinct symptom profiles to live triage model; assert explanations mention case symptoms and urgency rationale with anti-boilerplate checks across runs.

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (high same-day, medium within 48 hours).
behaviors.justification: Needs scheduling logic plus calendar data access working together. Rejected System: local DB-backed integration is sufficient; no OS/subprocess/real network dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Seed calendar DB with controlled availability; run scheduler for high and medium urgency; assert selected slot is earliest valid one within required window.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book a slot under concurrent patient processing.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is concurrency/transaction interaction between scheduler and shared calendar DB; module-only unit tests miss race behavior. Rejected System: can be caught with local real DB transaction semantics without external OS/network APIs.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests targeting same candidate slot against test DB with real locking/transactions; assert at most one booking succeeds for that slot.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-appropriate preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Primarily deterministic message composition/template rule logic; no required external boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation formatter with fixtures for visit types and appointment metadata; assert rendered message contains all required fields and correct instruction variant.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate -> insurance -> triage -> schedule -> confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to deterministic stubs and end-to-end module orchestration/state handoff. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because text disqualifies live model dependence ('stubbed triage output').
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline with stubbed insurance/triage outputs and real in-process module wiring; assert step order, state transitions, and final confirmation artifact.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Includes a real AI triage call in live pipeline; AI-CALL default applies, and completion depends on model-produced triage output flowing into downstream steps. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic decision stage is described, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake against live dependencies (payer API and live triage model) using a real submission; assert automated completion and persisted confirmation without human fallback.
